Model_20_MLB_Reliever_Spin_Rate_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB live-betting 8th/9th inning total runs by fading relief pitchers operating on zero days rest who heavily rely on high-spin breaking balls.
Model_28_NHL_Backup_Goalie_Rust.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NHL 1st Period OVERs by targeting backup goaltenders who are making their first start in over 14 days due to severe ring rust.
Model_14_MLB_Umpire_Variance.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB live-betting strikeout props and total runs by tracking the interaction between extreme pitch-framing catchers and umpires with historically wide strike zones.
Model_24_Tennis_Serve_Clock_Clay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live betting Tennis match totals and set spreads by targeting heavily-built baseline grinders on clay courts when chair umpires strictly enforce the serve clock during long rallies.
Model_18_NFL_Scripted_Drive_Efficiency.m: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL live-betting point spreads and 2nd Quarter totals by fading offensive units that rely heavily on highly orchestrated "scripted" opening drives but lack the ability to adapt once the script runs out.
Model_32_NHL_Penalty_Kill_Exhaustion.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NHL Live Betting 2nd Period Team Totals and Spreads by fading teams who take 3+ minor penalties in the 1st period, leading to severe 2nd period defensive breakdowns.
Model_23_NBA_Referee_Pace.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA 1st Quarter live betting point totals by targeting high-pace road teams playing on the second leg of a back-to-back against high-pace home teams when officiated by a crew known for high foul-call rates.
Model_27_NCAAB_Tournament_Fatigue.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in College Basketball Point Spreads by fading mid-major teams who are forced to play their 3rd game in 3 days during their conference tournament, especially when facing a higher-seeded team that received a double-bye (rested).
Model_30_NFL_Freezing_Kicker_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL Live Betting by fading long field goal attempts (>45 yards) in the 2nd half when the temperature drops below freezing.
Model_41_NFL_Primetime_Underdog.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL primetime (Sunday/Monday Night) point spreads by blindly backing the underdog when the underdog has a rookie head coach and the favorite has a veteran head coach.
Model_07_NHL_Empty_Net_Pulls.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NHL live-betting total goals over/under lines when a team trails by 2 goals in the 3rd period, by targeting coaches with a high statistical propensity for early goaltender pulls (3.5 to 5.0 minutes remaining).
Model_40_MLB_Catcher_Day_After_Night.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in opposing player stolen base props and pitcher strikeout props when a catcher is forced to start a Day game after playing a full Night game the previous evening.
Model_39_NBA_Ref_Home_Court.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live NBA moneyline and point spread betting by targeting home teams who are down 0-2 or 1-2 in a playoff series, capitalizing on the unconscious psychological bias of referees to extend the series ("series extender" bias) combined with the emotional pressure of a desperate home crowd.
Model_19_NCAAF_Service_Academy_Short_Res: Exploiting inefficiencies in College Football Team Total and Point Spread markets by fading defenses that are forced to prepare for a Service Academy (Army, Navy, Air Force) triple-option offense on short rest.
Model_42_NCAAF_Thursday_Night_Rivalry.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NCAAF Thursday Night games by fading highly-ranked road favorites against unranked home underdogs, capitalizing on unpriced travel logistics, missed classes, and the "spooky" atmosphere of weekday college towns.
Model_33_NCAAB_Blowout_Backdoor_Cover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in College Basketball Live Betting 2nd Half Spreads by fading heavily favored home teams who are leading by 20+ points at halftime against vastly inferior opponents.
Model_35_EPL_Derby_Ref_Leniency.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in English Premier League (EPL) Booking Points and Card markets by betting the UNDER on total booking points in highly anticipated, historically violent Derby matches when the appointed referee is notoriously lenient.
Model_77_Golf_Post_Major_Hangover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in PGA Tour 1st Round Matchup and 1st Round Leader markets by fading elite golfers who just contended heavily (Top 5 finish) in a grueling Major Championship the previous week. The market prices them based on their Official World Golf Ranking (OWGR) and recent "hot form," ignoring the profound mental exhaustion and adrenaline crash that follows a high-pressure Major Sunday.
Model_37_ATP_Baseline_Rally_Fatigue.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in ATP Tennis live betting moneylines and next-set winner markets by fading aging, baseline-reliant players in extreme heat following a marathon opening set.
Model_43_NFL_Green_Dot_Motion.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL point spreads and team totals by fading defenses that have lost their "Green Dot" middle linebacker against offenses that utilize extreme pre-snap motion.
Model_17_EPL_Var_Stoppage_Time_Surge.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live-betting prop markets (specifically Team Corners and Team Shots on Target) during the final 15 minutes of English Premier League matches following lengthy, controversial VAR reviews.
Model_31_NBA_Coach_Ejection_Surge.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA Live Betting 2nd Half Team Totals and Point Spreads by betting the OVER and ATS for a heavily-disadvantaged Home team immediately following the ejection of their Head Coach via double technicals in the late 2nd Quarter.
Model_44_Soccer_10_Man_Possession.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live soccer match betting when an elite possession-heavy team receives a red card in the first half but is currently leading 1-0, capitalizing on the unpriced reality that elite teams use possession as a defensive weapon.
Model_21_NHL_Goalie_Puck_Tracking.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live-betting NHL 3rd period total goals by targeting starting goaltenders operating on zero days rest who face a high volume of net-front traffic and screened shots.
Model_26_Golf_Wind_Asymmetry.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in Golf Matchups and Outright markets by fading elite golfers who are drawn into the afternoon wave when severe coastal wind spikes occur.
Model_45_NBA_Review_Momentum_Shift.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live NBA betting by fading teams on massive scoring runs immediately following a lengthy (>3 minute) referee review stoppage.
Model_25_EPL_European_Travel_Hangover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in EPL 1st Half Goal Totals and Team Totals by fading the offensive output of heavy-favorite teams playing on a Sunday afternoon immediately following a long away trip in European competition (Europa League/Conference League).
Model_38_NHL_Empty_Net_Desperation.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live NHL betting (Under 0.5 Empty Net Goals or "No" to Empty Net Goal) by fading empty net goals when the leading team is mathematically desperate for points in a tight playoff race, causing them to adopt an ultra-conservative, terrified defensive shell rather than aggressively shooting at the empty net.
Model_16_UFC_Altitude_Grappling_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in UFC live-betting moneylines and takedown props when fights take place at high altitude (e.g., Salt Lake City, Mexico City, Denver).
Model_79_MLB_Extra_Innings_Umpire.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB Total Runs (1st Inning / Full Game) by betting the Under the day after both teams played a grueling, 12+ inning marathon game that ended late at night. The market prices the next day's game based on starting pitchers and bullpen fatigue, but ignores the extreme fatigue of the Home Plate Umpire, which physically alters the strike zone.
Model_22_NFL_TNF_West_To_East.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL 1st Half Point Spread and Total markets by fading West Coast teams traveling two or more time zones East to play on Thursday Night Football (short week).
Model_15_NBA_B2B_Travel_3PT_Variance.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA player 3-point shooting props and team totals when a team plays the second night of a back-to-back (B2B) involving travel across two or more time zones.
Model_36_NFL_Preseason_QB_Rotation.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL Preseason 2nd Half and 4th Quarter point spreads and moneylines by fading teams rotating in pure rookie quarterbacks against veteran "journeyman" backups.
Model_29_NBA_B2B_Rebound_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA Player Prop markets by betting the UNDER on Total Rebounds for starting Centers who are playing the second leg of a back-to-back against elite offensive-rebounding opponents.
Model_78_NFL_Weather_Dome_Outdoors.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL Spread and Total markets during November/December by fading elite Dome/Warm-Weather teams when they travel to play in freezing, inclement weather. The market mathematically adjusts the Total downwards for the weather but often fails to adequately penalize the *offensive efficiency* of the Dome team, assuming their superstar QB can overcome the elements.
Model_34_MLB_Bullpen_Game_Altitude.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB Live Betting total runs and team totals by fading pitching staffs executing a "Bullpen Game" (using 5+ relief pitchers) when playing in extreme high-altitude environments like Coors Field.
Model_71_NBA_Late_Scratch_Usage.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA Player Prop markets when a superstar player is ruled out ("late scratch") 15 minutes before tip-off. The betting market algorithmically bumps up the points/rebounds/assists (PRA) props of the remaining secondary scorers based on projected "Usage Rate" increases. However, the market fails to account for the catastrophic drop in offensive efficiency when secondary scorers are forced into primary shot-creation roles against set defenses.
Model_51_ATP_Heat_Index_Serve_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in live tennis betting (specifically fading the server's hold percentage in the 2nd and 3rd sets) when heavily muscled, tall "servebots" play in extreme heat/humidity. The betting market prices their serve based on their baseline averages, ignoring the catastrophic decay of fast-twitch core muscles required to generate high ace rates in thermal shock conditions.
Model_70_UFC_Short_Notice_Weight_Cut.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in UFC Live Betting and Round Prop markets by fading fighters who accept a bout on less than 2 weeks' notice and are forced into an extreme, acute weight cut (>15 lbs). The pre-fight market often prices the fight based on historical talent, but live markets fail to rapidly adjust to the catastrophic cardiovascular failure that occurs precisely halfway through the second round due to brain fluid depletion.
Model_46_NHL_Goalie_Shutout_Hangover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NHL live betting markets and period-specific totals by fading a goalie immediately following a 40+ save shutout performance in their previous game.
Model_47_NCAAF_Air_Raid_Wind.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NCAAF totals and point spreads by fading "Air-Raid" or pure pass-heavy offenses in extreme crosswind conditions.
Model_48_NFL_Altitude_B2B_Road.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL 4th quarter live betting markets and game totals by fading teams playing their second consecutive road game at high altitude (Denver).
Model_54_MLB_Cold_Weather_Velocity.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB pitcher strikeout props and moneylines in the first month of the season (April) by fading elite power pitchers (high velocity, high spin) when they pitch in freezing temperatures. The market prices them based on their true-talent baseline, failing to account for the mechanical degradation of grip friction on hardened baseballs in the cold.
Model_76_WNBA_Super_Team_Fatigue.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in WNBA Total Points and Spread markets by fading elite "Super-Teams" when they play the second leg of a back-to-back, specifically when the second game requires flying into high altitude (Denver/Colorado or similar environments, or brutal cross-country commercial travel). The market prices the Super-Team purely on mathematical talent and offensive rating, failing to account for the extreme physical exhaustion compounded by commercial travel and lack of oxygen.
Model_74_F1_Post_Safety_Car_Tire_Fade.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in Formula 1 Live Betting (Lap-by-Lap Matchups / Podium Finishes) immediately following a prolonged Safety Car period. The market prices drivers based on track position and overall car pace, but fails to account for extreme disparities in tire temperature generation between different chassis philosophies on the restart lap.
Model_67_Soccer_Post_International_Break: Exploiting inefficiencies in European Domestic Soccer (Premier League, La Liga, Serie A) Asian Handicap and Goal Line markets by fading elite clubs in their first match immediately following a FIFA International Break. The market prices the elite club based on their overall talent and historical dominance, failing to account for the asymmetric fatigue and injury risk absorbed by clubs who send 15+ players across the globe compared to mid-table opponents who rested for two weeks.
Model_75_CBB_Rivalry_Hangover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NCAA Men's Basketball Spread markets by fading teams who just secured a massive, emotionally exhausting upset victory over an intense in-state rival, and are now playing a trap game on the road against an unranked conference opponent 48 hours later. The market heavily upgrades their power rating based on the high-profile win, ignoring the acute dopamine depletion and physical fatigue of college athletes.
Model_72_EPL_Relegation_Motivation.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in English Premier League (EPL) Asian Handicap markets during the final 6 weeks of the season (April/May) by betting on desperate teams fighting relegation when they play comfortable mid-table teams who have "nothing to play for" ("on the beach"). The market prices the game based on full-season Expected Goals (xG) metrics, fundamentally ignoring the psychological extremes of survival desperation versus vacation anticipation.
Model_68_MLB_Bullpen_Blowup.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB Live Betting and Late-Inning Run Line markets by fading teams whose top 3 high-leverage relief pitchers have all pitched in 3 of the last 4 days. The market prices the team's overall bullpen ERA based on season-long averages, failing to account for the catastrophic drop in velocity and command caused by acute, localized arm fatigue.
Model_55_NBA_All_Star_Lookahead.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA point spreads and player props in the final game before the All-Star Break by fading young, eliminated or non-playoff contention teams playing on the road. The market prices the game based on seasonal net rating, failing to account for the catastrophic drop in effort from young players whose minds are already on their vacation flights to Miami or Cabo.
Model_62_NBA_Altitude_B2B.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA Moneyline, Spread, and Player Prop markets by fading teams playing the second night of a back-to-back (B2B) in high-altitude cities (Denver or Utah) after playing at sea level the night before. The market applies a standard "B2B penalty," but fails to properly weight the exponential fatigue multiplier caused by combining sleep deprivation with severe oxygen debt.
Model_57_PGA_East_Coast_Travel.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in PGA Tour "To Make the Cut" and Round 1 Matchup markets during the "Florida Swing" (early Spring). Specifically fading West Coast-native or West Coast-based golfers when they make their first start on the East Coast (Florida) after playing the "West Coast Swing" (California/Arizona). The market prices them based on their recent form out West, completely ignoring the drastic agronomic shift and time-zone fatigue.
Model_52_UFC_Leg_Kick_Takedown_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in UFC live-betting takedown props and moneylines by fading heavily muscled, explosive wrestlers after they absorb massive damage to their lead leg (calf kicks), specifically targeting the 2nd and 3rd rounds. The market prices the wrestler's takedown ability based on historical averages, ignoring the catastrophic biomechanical failure required to shoot a takedown with a deadened lead leg.
Model_64_F1_Monaco_Engine_Degradation.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in Formula 1 "To Retire from Race" and Head-to-Head matchup markets by fading teams/drivers utilizing older power unit (PU) components in the race immediately following the Monaco Grand Prix. The market prices reliability based on overall season averages, failing to account for the unique, severe thermal degradation the Monaco circuit inflicts on internal combustion engines.
Model_63_ATP_Grand_Slam_Hangover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in ATP Men's Tennis Moneyline and Set Spread markets by fading mid-tier players who are playing a minor ATP 250 tournament immediately following a "career-best" deep run (Quarterfinal or Semifinal) in a Grand Slam. The market prices them at peak rating based on their Grand Slam performance, ignoring the massive emotional letdown and physical exhaustion.
Model_69_NFL_Lookahead_Sandwich.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL Spread and Team Total markets by fading elite Super Bowl-contending teams when they play a massive underdog divisional opponent, sandwiched immediately before a highly anticipated, prime-time matchup against another Super Bowl contender. The market prices the game purely on mathematical talent disparity, ignoring the profound human psychological element of "looking ahead."
Model_59_UFC_Altitude_Cardio.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in UFC Moneyline and "Fight Goes to Decision" markets for events held at extreme high altitude (e.g., Salt Lake City, Mexico City). Fading heavy, muscle-bound fighters who rely on explosive power when fighting at altitude. The market prices fighters based on their sea-level cardio metrics, failing to account for the catastrophic oxygen debt experienced by heavily muscled fighters above 4,000 feet.
Model_58_NHL_West_Coast_Road_Trip.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NHL Moneyline, Puckline, and Team Total markets by heavily fading Eastern Conference teams playing the final game of a grueling "California/Western Canada" road trip. The market prices the game based on seasonal analytics, completely failing to capture the compounding physical toll of playing 3 or 4 games in 6 nights across 3 time zones.
Model_56_EPL_Intl_Break_Jetlag.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in English Premier League (EPL) player prop markets and Team Totals in the first match immediately following an International Break. Specifically fading star players from South America or Africa who played full 90-minute international matches on Wednesday night and are forced to play in the Saturday 12:30 PM (early kickoff) EPL fixture. The market prices them at their true-talent baseline, failing to account for the massive 10+ hour transatlantic flights, time-zone jet lag, and total lack of club training.
Model_50_MLB_Umpire_Travel_Hangover.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB pitcher strikeout props and live team totals by fading starting pitchers when the home plate umpire is operating on severe sleep deprivation due to Sunday Night Baseball travel schedules or cross-country flight hangovers. The market prices the pitcher's stuff, but fails to account for the catastrophic shrinking of the umpire's strike zone due to deteriorated fast-twitch visual tracking.
Model_53_NFL_Dome_Outdoor_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL Team Totals and point spreads by fading heavily dome-reliant, warm-weather passing offenses when they are forced to travel to outdoor, freezing-temperature stadiums late in the season (December/January). The market consistently underestimates the catastrophic degradation of timing-based passing attacks in dense, freezing air.
Model_49_NBA_Late_Game_FT_Pressure.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA live-betting Team Totals and Player Prop Unders by targeting players at the free-throw line in clutch, high-pressure situations (score within 5 points, under 2 minutes remaining) where the market assumes standard career FT%.
Model_60_MLB_Catchers_Day_After_Night.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB Player Props (Hits, Total Bases) and Team Stolen Bases by fading starting catchers who are forced to play a day game immediately following a night game (often on Thursday or Sunday "Getaway Days"). The market prices their offensive and defensive props at their seasonal averages, failing to account for the severe physical toll of catching 150+ pitches on less than 12 hours of rest.
Model_61_NFL_TNF_Road_Dog_Exhaustion.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL Point Spreads and Team Totals by heavily fading West Coast underdog teams forced to travel cross-country to play on the East Coast on a short week for Thursday Night Football. The market adjusts the spread by standard home-field advantage metrics, failing to appropriately price the compounding effect of physical trauma recovery compressed into 3 days plus a transatlantic/cross-country flight.
Model_73_Tennis_Challenger_Transition.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in ATP Tennis 1st Round Matchup and Set Betting markets by fading young prospects who just won a lower-tier Challenger Tour title the previous week and immediately transition to an ATP 250/500 main draw. The market heavily overvalues their "current form" and recent win streak, failing to account for the massive jump in opponent quality and the accumulated physical/mental fatigue of a deep run in a minor tournament.
Model_65_NHL_Post_West_Coast_Road_Trip.m: Exploiting inefficiencies in NHL Moneyline and Team Total markets by fading Eastern Conference teams playing their first home game back immediately after a grueling 4+ game West Coast road trip (e.g., California/Western Canada swing). The market generally prices returning home as a positive ("Home Ice Advantage"), failing to account for the severe circadian rhythm disruption and the physical letdown of returning to domestic distractions after living in a hotel for two weeks.
Model_66_PGA_Bermuda_to_Poa_Annua.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in PGA Tour Tournament Matchups and "To Miss Cut" markets by fading golfers who have elite putting statistics exclusively on Bermuda grass greens when they transition to the West Coast swing tournaments played on Poa Annua grass (e.g., Pebble Beach, Torrey Pines, Riviera). The market prices overall Strokes Gained: Putting as a universal skill, ignoring extreme surface-specific splits.
Model_05_NBA_Altitude_Fatigue.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NBA Player Props (specifically 4th Quarter Points and Rebounds) when an opposing team is playing the second night of a back-to-back (B2B) and travels to a high-altitude arena (Denver/Utah).
Model_01_Late_Game_Poisson.md:
Searching for statistical anomalies in late-game scoring probabilities (80th+ minute) when a heavy favorite is down by 1 goal. 
**Layer 1:** Simple Poisson distribution anomalies.
**Layer 2:** Simple Poisson fails due to the independence assumption and constant rate. Layered complexity requires replacing static historical $\lambda$ with a **Game-State Adjusted $\lambda$** calculated from live match Expected Goals (xG). The model must now calculate if the trailing team's offensive surge outpaces the leading team's counter-attack capability, comparing this refined $\lambda$ against bookmaker fair odds.
**Layer 3 (Added today):** Monte Carlo simulation reveals that the edge on betting the trailing team to equalize is systematically destroyed by counter-attack vulnerability (Exposure Factor). The trailing team's EV is -0.115, whereas the leading team's counter-attack EV is +1.094. Pivot the model: Do not bet on the trailing team to score; instead, bet on the *leading* team to score the next goal via counter-attack when the trailing team over-commits men forward.
**Layer 4 (Added today):** Simulated the effects of time decay and "Rest Defense" structures. Expected Goals (xG) systematically undervalues defensive positioning on counter-attacks. However, the EV holds at +0.42 net of vig *only* when the trailing team has >65% possession but very low shot volume (sterile domination), making them highly vulnerable to counter-attacks in the final 5 minutes.
**Layer 5 (Added today):** Modeled opposition defensive shape. If the trailing team utilizes a "low block" (vertical compactness < 13m), transition distance increases, and counter-attack EV drops to -0.02. The model must explicitly filter for trailing teams pushing a high defensive line.
Model_06_NFL_Wind_Decay.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in NFL live-betting passing yards/completion props by tracking unpriced micro-weather shifts (specifically wind speed increases) occurring in the 2nd half of open-air stadium games.
Model_04_Umpire_Catcher_Synergy.md: Exploiting inefficiencies in MLB Totals (Over/Under) and Pitcher Strikeout Props by quantifying the symbiotic relationship between home-plate umpires and catcher framing in the "Shadow Zone." Public models weigh pitcher K% heavily, but often under-price the compounding effect of an elite framing catcher paired with a historically "pitcher-friendly" umpire with a wide zone.
Model_13_Tennis_Serve_Clock.md: Exploiting live-betting momentum swings in ATP Tennis matches based on the strict enforcement of the newly automated 25-second serve clock rule.
Model_02_Meteorological_Edges.md:
Analyzing if the betting market systematically under-prices extreme wind or rain conditions in specific open-stadium leagues.
**To Do:** Identify historical meteorological data sources and cross-reference with closing line value.
Model_03_Momentum_Reversion.md:
Testing if live-betting algorithms overreact to momentum shifts (e.g., being broken in the second set after winning the first).
**Layer 1:** Basic momentum shift overreaction.
**Layer 2:** Evolved to explicitly separate ATP vs. WTA market dynamics. ATP momentum reversion relies heavily on service dominance stability, while WTA momentum relies on return efficiency volatility. We must distinguish a true momentum shift from a "temporary spike in unforced errors." The model must trigger buys when heavy favorites drop a set or get broken purely due to a short-term isolated cluster of unforced errors, exploiting algorithm panic.
**Layer 3 (Added today):** Simulated break-back probabilities reveal that momentum reversion fails completely on Grass surfaces (prob of breaking back drops to ~12.5%, destroying EV). The model must explicitly filter out all Grass and Fast Hard court events, and restrict betting strictly to Clay or Slow Hard courts where break-back probability sustains at ~26.4%.
